title: Execution Model
text:
[REQ-0048] The library MUST run its event loop on a dedicated OS thread spawned at Agent construction time.
[REQ-0049] The library MUST NOT require an async runtime.

[REQ-0050] The event loop MUST manage a TCP listener for inbound TLS connections and a UDP socket for outbound trap sending.
**Exception (REQ-0050):** TLS wrapping of the TCP listener is deferred to RFC-0006:C-TRANSPORT.
The TCP listener and UDP socket are in place; the TLS layer is out of scope for this RFC.

[REQ-0051] The event loop MUST accept incoming TCP connections, perform TLS handshakes, and read framed SNMPv3 messages from established TLS connections.
**Exception (REQ-0051):** TLS handshakes are deferred to RFC-0006:C-TRANSPORT.
The event loop accepts TCP connections and reads framed SNMPv3 messages over plain TCP; the TLS layer is out of scope for this RFC.

[REQ-0052] The event loop MUST drain a command channel between socket polls to process application requests (MIB updates, trap sends).

[REQ-0053] The library MUST NOT perform blocking operations that could stall the event loop.
[REQ-0054] Any long-running work MUST be kept off the event loop thread.

**Rationale:**
A dedicated OS thread with a synchronous event loop is the simplest execution model that avoids the complexity of an async runtime for a low-throughput SNMP workload.
Owning all state on a single thread eliminates the need for locking.
A command channel provides the boundary between application threads and the event loop.
